description_long: This is a medium-sized, round ceramic bowl with a wide, slightly flared rim and a gently curving body that tapers toward the base. The surface is matte and shows a natural, earthy brown coloration with some lighter patches and visible wear, suggesting age or frequent use. The rim is uneven and slightly wavy, giving the bowl a handmade appearance. There are a few small chips and surface abrasions, as well as some faint residue or markings, possibly inventory labels, on one side. The interior appears to be similar in texture and color to the exterior, with no visible glaze. Overall, the bowl has a utilitarian, rustic look typical of traditional or archaeological kitchenware.
description: A rustic, brown ceramic bowl with a wide, flared rim and visible signs of wear and age. The surface is matte and uneven, with some chips and patches.
description_view_0: The bowl is seen from a side angle, highlighting its flared rim and gently curving body with a mostly smooth, matte brown surface.
description_view_1: This view shows the bowl's side with two visible inventory labels and more pronounced surface wear and discoloration.
description_view_2: The bowl is viewed head-on, showing the full circular opening and the evenness of the rim, with some surface patches visible.
description_view_3: Another side view, showing a few small chips and lighter patches on the surface, with the rim's unevenness more apparent.
attribute_context_of_use: serving or holding food, possibly for everyday or historical use

license_info.creator_display_name: The Royal Museums of Art and History
